subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
question_text:
Given below are names of some animals:
(i) Goat (ii) Human beings (iii) Cockroach (iv) Eagle
Which of the above animals form a pair of omnivores?
answer_explanation: Human beings and cockroaches are omnivores — they eat both plants and animals.

subject: Science

question_text:
Below are names of some animals:
(i) Cow (ii) Sheep (iii) Horse (iv) Ox
Which of the above are sources of milk for human beings?
answer_explanation: Cow and sheep are common milk-producing animals used by humans.

question_text:
Given below is a list of edible plants:

Which pair of plants have two or more edible parts?

question_text:
Read the clues and fill up the blanks:
(a) Honeybees suck _____ from flowers.
(b) Animals which eat other animals: _____
(c) Animals which eat only plants: _____
(d) Animals which eat both plants and animals: _____
answer_explanation: Basic classification of animals based on food habits.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
question_text: Why do boiled seeds fail to sprout?
answer_key: Boiling kills the embryo (living part) of the seed, so it cannot germinate.
answer_explanation: The heat denatures proteins and kills cells in the seed.
provenance_excerpt: Why do boiled seeds fail to sprout?

subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
answer_explanation: Honeybees build wax honeycombs to store honey.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
question_text: Name two ingredients in our food that are not obtained from plants or animals. Mention one source for each ingredient.
answer_key: Salt — obtained from sea water/rock deposits. Water — obtained from rivers, wells, rain.
answer_explanation: Salt and water are mineral/inorganic ingredients.
provenance_excerpt: Name two ingredients in our food that are not obtained from plants or animals. Mention one source for each ingredient.

answer_key: Herbivores: deer, rabbit. Carnivores: lion, tiger. Omnivores: bear, crow.

subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
question_text: Why should we avoid wastage of food?
answer_key: We should avoid food wastage because: (1) Many people don't get enough food. (2) Growing food requires resources like water, fertilisers, and labour. (3) Wasting food means wasting these resources.
answer_explanation: Food wastage has social and environmental impacts.
provenance_excerpt: Why should we avoid wastage of food?

subject: Science
chapter_title: Food: Where Does It Come From?
question_text: Why do organisms need food? Write two reasons.
answer_key: (1) Food provides energy for life processes like growth, repair, and movement. (2) Food provides nutrients needed to build and repair body parts.
answer_explanation: Food is essential for energy and growth.
provenance_excerpt: Why do organisms need food? Write two reasons.
